basic_details.job_title: Rajasthan RSSB CET Senior Secondary Level Notification 2026 Out - Apply Online for Group C Posts
basic_details.post_name: 14 services (Vanpal, Constable, Junior Assistant, Clerk Grade-II, etc.)
basic_details.short_information: Rajasthan Staff Selection Board (RSSB) has officially released the Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary Level 2026 notification (Advertisement No. 08/2026). This is a Common Eligibility Test for 14 services across Rajasthan state departments. The online application window opens 24 June 2026 and closes 23 July 2026 at 11:59 PM. The exam is scheduled 23–25 October 2026 (offline OMR-based). Candidates must score a minimum of 40% (35% for SC/ST) to qualify.

application_fee.details: The fee for Rajasthan CET 2026 is collected as a One Time Registration (OTR) charge through the SSO Portal. Candidates who have already paid OTR fees in a previous cycle do not need to pay again. Payments can be made through state-authorised e-Mitra kiosks, Jan Suvidha Kendras (CSC), net banking, ATM-cum-debit cards, or credit cards. | Candidates from other states belonging to SC/ST/OBC/MBC/EWS categories are treated as General category and must pay ₹600. If an applicant remains absent in two or more exams in a financial year (1 April–31 March) across RPSC, RSSB, or any other state recruitment agency, their OTR facility will be blocked; reactivation requires a penalty fee of ₹750 (first instance) or ₹1,500 (second instance).

age_limit.details: According to the Personnel Department notification dated 23 May 2022 and 13 October 2022, the educational qualification required to appear in the CET (Senior Secondary Level) is Senior Secondary (Class 12th) or its equivalent. Candidates appearing in their final year of Senior Secondary may also apply, but must acquire the qualification as per the relevant departmental service rules. | The specific educational qualification, experience, and other eligibility conditions for each included post will be governed by the applicable service rules of that department. These will be detailed in the department-wise main recruitment notification. | Candidates must be at least 18 years old and not more than 40 years old as on 1 January 2027 to be eligible for Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary Level 2026. | Age Relaxation: | Candidates wondering about multiple age relaxation benefits should note that the relaxations listed above are non-cumulative - only one category's relaxation applies at a time.

qualification.details: According to the Personnel Department notification dated 23 May 2022 and 13 October 2022, the educational qualification required to appear in the CET (Senior Secondary Level) is Senior Secondary ( Class 12th ) or its equivalent . Candidates appearing in their final year of Senior Secondary may also apply, but must acquire the qualification as per the relevant departmental service rules. | The specific educational qualification, experience, and other eligibility conditions for each included post will be governed by the applicable service rules of that department. These will be detailed in the department-wise main recruitment notification.

selection_process: The Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary Level 2026 is only a qualifying/eligibility examination. After the CET
the actual recruitment for each post is conducted through a separate departmental process. Candidates should understand that clearing CET is the first step in a multi-stage recruitment cycle.
Stage 1 – CET (Common Eligibility Test) An OMR-based offline exam of 120 questions carrying 240 marks
to be held 23–25 October 2026. Duration is 2 hours 30 minutes. Each wrong answer carries a negative mark of 1/3 of the marks allotted to the question. Each question has five options (A
E); if a candidate does not attempt a question
otherwise 1/3 marks will be deducted for non-darkening in more than 10% of questions.
Stage 2 – Departmental Main Recruitment Exam Once the Board announces vacancy-wise recruitment
eligible CET scorers must appear in a departmental written test or interview (or both) conducted by the respective recruiting agency as per applicable service rules.
Stage 3 – Document Verification and Medical Category-wise merit-listed candidates undergo document verification and medical examination before final appointment.
If the Board conducts the recruitment exam in more than one shift or session
normalization of scores will be applied.

pattern_table.Subject: History of Rajasthan, Art & Culture, Geography of India, Geography of Rajasthan, Indian Polity (with special reference to Rajasthan), Economy of Rajasthan, Everyday Science, Logical Reasoning & Mathematics, Current Affairs, Public Health, Computer Knowledge, General Hindi, General English

exam_pattern.details: All questions are multiple choice with equal marks. Negative marking of 1/3 marks per wrong answer applies.

syllabus.details: History & Culture of Rajasthan: Ancient civilizations, prominent rulers, 1857 revolt, peasant and tribal movements, Praja Mandal, integration of Rajasthan, architecture, folk music, dance, theatre, religious sects, folk deities, languages and dialects. | Geography (India & Rajasthan): Physical features, major rivers, dams, lakes, natural resources (mineral, forest, water), wildlife, tribes, tourism, climate, population statistics. | Indian Polity with Rajasthan Reference: Constitution, Fundamental Rights, DPSP, President, Prime Minister, Parliament, Supreme Court, Election Commission, Governor, Chief Minister, State Legislature, High Court, RPSC, Panchayati Raj, local self-government. | Economy of Rajasthan: Industry, agriculture, animal husbandry, minerals, state income, budget concepts, handicraft industry, welfare schemes, MGNREGA, VB-G RAM G Mission. | Everyday Science: Physical/chemical changes, metals/non-metals, carbon compounds, light, space technology, genetics, ecosystems, biotechnology, blood groups, human diseases. | Logical Reasoning & Mathematics: Blood relations, number/alphabet series, clock, calendar, LCM/HCF, average, profit/loss, percentage, simple and compound interest, ratio/proportion, time-speed-distance, areas, volumes, data representation, coding-decoding, seating arrangement. | Current Affairs: State and national events, famous personalities, state programs and policies, civic duties, Rajasthan Public Examination (Prevention of Unfair Means in Recruitment) Act 2022. | Public Health: First aid basics, CPR, drug abuse prevention, youth fitness, social media health risks. | Computer Knowledge: Computer characteristics, RAM/ROM, file system, input/output devices, MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint). | General Hindi & General English: Grammar, comprehension, vocabulary, official letter writing, translations.

how_to_apply: The online application for Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary Level 2026 is accepted only through the SSO Portal at sso.rajasthan.gov.in. No offline applications will be accepted under any circumstances. Candidates are advised to read the complete notification before starting the application.
Visit http://sso.rajasthan.gov.in and log in using your SSO ID.
Under Citizen Apps (G2C), select the Recruitment Portal.
Click on Apply Now for CET Senior Secondary Level 2026.
If you have not yet completed OTR (One Time Registration), go to the OTR tab first and select your category (UR/Reserved), disability status, and home state, then pay the prescribed OTR fee.
Complete OTR carefully - details entered here (category, disability, home state) will be auto-populated in the application and cannot be edited easily.

Upload Live Photo via OTR (auto-fetched by the system through the OTR dashboard camera). Ensure a clear face covering at least 50% of the frame, with white/light background, no dark glasses, and no covered head.
Upload scanned Signature (Hindi + English) in a 7 cm × 2 cm box using black/dark blue pen on white A4 paper - JPEG format, 280×80 to 560×160 pixels, file size 20–50 KB.
Upload Left Thumb Impression (or right thumb if left is unavailable) separately - same JPEG specifications, max 50 KB.
Fill in all educational and recruitment-related details carefully in the application form.
Click Final Submit. An Application ID (Application Number) will be generated - save the printout.
Important: The application portal link deactivates after 23 July 2026 at 23:59 hrs. Candidates should not use another person's SSO ID. An application without an Application ID is not accepted.

content_sections.heading: Rajasthan CET 2026 Senior Secondary Level Notification Out
content_sections.text: Candidates who appeared in the previous CET (Senior Secondary Level) 2024 must re-apply, as that exam's score validity expired on 16 February 2026 - one year from the result date. Only the best available score within the validity period is considered for upcoming departmental recruitment exams. Download Rajasthan CET 2026 Senior Secondary Level Notification PDF

content_sections.heading: Rajasthan CET 2026 Senior Secondary Level Recruitment 2026 - Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates planning to apply for Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary 2026 must note all key dates carefully. The application portal goes live on 24 June 2026 and remains open until 23 July 2026 . Applicants are advised not to wait for the last date to avoid technical delays. The Board reserves the right to change the exam dates. All updates will be published exclusively on the official website; no separate letters will be sent to candidates. content_sections.heading: Eligibility – Qualification and Age Limit
content_sections.text: According to the Personnel Department notification dated 23 May 2022 and 13 October 2022, the educational qualification required to appear in the CET (Senior Secondary Level) is Senior Secondary ( Class 12th ) or its equivalent . Candidates appearing in their final year of Senior Secondary may also apply, but must acquire the qualification as per the relevant departmental service rules. The specific educational qualification, experience, and other eligibility conditions for each included post will be governed by the applicable service rules of that department. These will be detailed in the department-wise main recruitment notification. Candidates must be at least 18 years old and not more than 40 years old as on 1 January 2027 to be eligible for Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary Level 2026. Age Relaxation: Candidates wondering about multiple age relaxation benefits should note that the relaxations listed above are non-cumulative - only one category's relaxation applies at a time.

rows.relaxation: 5 years additional (over and above category relaxation)
Up to 15 years (maximum upper age 50 years; 55 years in direct recruitment requiring lower post experience)
No upper age limit (subject to state retirement age)

content_sections.heading: Application Fee (OTR One Time Registration)
content_sections.text: The fee for Rajasthan CET 2026 is collected as a One Time Registration (OTR) charge through the SSO Portal. Candidates who have already paid OTR fees in a previous cycle do not need to pay again. Payments can be made through state-authorised e-Mitra kiosks, Jan Suvidha Kendras (CSC), net banking, ATM-cum-debit cards, or credit cards. Candidates from other states belonging to SC/ST/OBC/MBC/EWS categories are treated as General category and must pay ₹600. If an applicant remains absent in two or more exams in a financial year (1 April–31 March) across RPSC, RSSB, or any other state recruitment agency, their OTR facility will be blocked; reactivation requires a penalty fee of ₹750 (first instance) or ₹1,500 (second instance).

rows.category: Non-Creamy Layer OBC / MBC (Rajasthan) + EWS + SC / ST (Rajasthan)

content_sections.heading: Minimum Qualifying Marks
content_sections.text: Candidates who wish to be eligible for departmental selection must score a minimum of 40% marks in the CET. SC/ST candidates are eligible for a relaxation of up to 5 percentage points , i.e., a minimum of 35%. Divyang candidates are entitled to a 5% relaxation in the minimum qualifying marks as per Rajasthan Divyangjan Rights (Amendment) Rules 2021, Rule 6(B). Scoring above 40% in the CET does not guarantee a job; it only makes a candidate eligible to appear in the separate departmental recruitment exam (written test and/or interview) for specific posts.

content_sections.heading: Selection Process
content_sections.text: The Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary Level 2026 is only a qualifying/eligibility examination. After the CET, the actual recruitment for each post is conducted through a separate departmental process. Candidates should understand that clearing CET is the first step in a multi-stage recruitment cycle. Stage 1 – CET (Common Eligibility Test) An OMR-based offline exam of 120 questions carrying 240 marks, to be held 23–25 October 2026. Duration is 2 hours 30 minutes. Each wrong answer carries a negative mark of 1/3 of the marks allotted to the question. Each question has five options (A, B, C, D, E) ; if a candidate does not attempt a question, they must darken option E, otherwise 1/3 marks will be deducted for non-darkening in more than 10% of questions. Stage 2 – Departmental Main Recruitment Exam Once the Board announces vacancy-wise recruitment, eligible CET scorers must appear in a departmental written test or interview (or both) conducted by the respective recruiting agency as per applicable service rules. Stage 3 – Document Verification and Medical Category-wise merit-listed candidates undergo document verification and medical examination before final appointment. If the Board conducts the recruitment exam in more than one shift or session, normalization of scores will be applied.

content_sections.heading: Rajasthan CET 2026 Senior Secondary Level Exam Date 2026
content_sections.text: Rajasthan CET 2026 Senior Secondary Level exam date has been officially announced by the Rajasthan Staff Selection Board. The examination will be conducted from October 23 to October 25, 2026. This update is important for candidates preparing for Rajasthan CET 12th level recruitment, as it helps them plan their final revision and exam strategy. The exam will be held across various centres in Rajasthan, and candidates should stay updated for admit card release and other official instructions. Check Rajasthan CET Exam Date 2026

content_sections.heading: Exam Pattern and Syllabus
content_sections.text: All questions are multiple choice with equal marks. Negative marking of 1/3 marks per wrong answer applies.

rows.subject: History of Rajasthan, Art & Culture, Geography of India, Geography of Rajasthan, Indian Polity (with special reference to Rajasthan), Economy of Rajasthan, Everyday Science, Logical Reasoning & Mathematics, Current Affairs, Public Health, Computer Knowledge, General Hindi, General English

content_sections.heading: Key Syllabus Topics
content_sections.text: History & Culture of Rajasthan: Ancient civilizations, prominent rulers, 1857 revolt, peasant and tribal movements, Praja Mandal, integration of Rajasthan, architecture, folk music, dance, theatre, religious sects, folk deities, languages and dialects. Geography (India & Rajasthan): Physical features, major rivers, dams, lakes, natural resources (mineral, forest, water), wildlife, tribes, tourism, climate, population statistics. Indian Polity with Rajasthan Reference: Constitution, Fundamental Rights, DPSP, President, Prime Minister, Parliament, Supreme Court, Election Commission, Governor, Chief Minister, State Legislature, High Court, RPSC, Panchayati Raj, local self-government. Economy of Rajasthan: Industry, agriculture, animal husbandry, minerals, state income, budget concepts, handicraft industry, welfare schemes, MGNREGA, VB-G RAM G Mission. Everyday Science: Physical/chemical changes, metals/non-metals, carbon compounds, light, space technology, genetics, ecosystems, biotechnology, blood groups, human diseases. Logical Reasoning & Mathematics: Blood relations, number/alphabet series, clock, calendar, LCM/HCF, average, profit/loss, percentage, simple and compound interest, ratio/proportion, time-speed-distance, areas, volumes, data representation, coding-decoding, seating arrangement. Current Affairs: State and national events, famous personalities, state programs and policies, civic duties, Rajasthan Public Examination (Prevention of Unfair Means in Recruitment) Act 2022. Public Health: First aid basics, CPR, drug abuse prevention, youth fitness, social media health risks. Computer Knowledge: Computer characteristics, RAM/ROM, file system, input/output devices, MS Office (Word, Excel, PowerPoint). General Hindi & General English: Grammar, comprehension, vocabulary, official letter writing, translations.

content_sections.heading: How to Apply – Rajasthan CET 2026 Apply Online
content_sections.text: The online application for Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary Level 2026 is accepted only through the SSO Portal at sso.rajasthan.gov.in . No offline applications will be accepted under any circumstances. Candidates are advised to read the complete notification before starting the application. Step-by-Step Application Process: Visit http://sso.rajasthan.gov.in and log in using your SSO ID. Under Citizen Apps (G2C), select the Recruitment Portal . Click on Apply Now for CET Senior Secondary Level 2026. If you have not yet completed OTR (One Time Registration) , go to the OTR tab first and select your category (UR/Reserved), disability status, and home state, then pay the prescribed OTR fee. Complete OTR carefully - details entered here (category, disability, home state) will be auto-populated in the application and cannot be edited easily. Upload Live Photo via OTR (auto-fetched by the system through the OTR dashboard camera). Ensure a clear face covering at least 50% of the frame, with white/light background, no dark glasses, and no covered head. Upload scanned Signature (Hindi + English) in a 7 cm × 2 cm box using black/dark blue pen on white A4 paper - JPEG format, 280×80 to 560×160 pixels, file size 20–50 KB. Upload Left Thumb Impression (or right thumb if left is unavailable) separately - same JPEG specifications, max 50 KB. Fill in all educational and recruitment-related details carefully in the application form. Click Final Submit . An Application ID (Application Number) will be generated - save the printout. Important: The application portal link deactivates after 23 July 2026 at 23:59 hrs . Candidates should not use another person's SSO ID. An application without an Application ID is not accepted.

content_sections.heading: Rajasthan CET 2026 Apply Online
content_sections.text: The direct link to apply online for Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary Level 2026 is available on the Recruitment Portal at https://recruitment.rajasthan.gov.in . Candidates must log in through their own SSO ID. The portal will remain active from 24 June 2026 to 23 July 2026 . After Final Submit, candidates can make corrections during the permitted editing window. A correction fee of ₹300 is required for changes made within 3 days after the application window closes. A second and final correction opportunity is available for 7 days after the exam, covering all fields except the candidate's name, father's name, date of birth, photograph, signature, thumb impression, and handwriting sample. Apply Online (SSO Portal): http://sso.rajasthan.gov.in

content_sections.heading: Admit Card
content_sections.text: All admit cards for Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary Level 2026 will be issued online through SSO Login and at the Recruitment Portal link https://recruitment.rajasthan.gov.in/rectlogingetadmitcard . No admit card will be sent by post. Candidates should keep their Application ID and SSO ID handy to download the admit card when it is released. The admit card release date will be announced via newspapers and the official RSSB website.

content_sections.heading: Reservation Policy
content_sections.text: Rajasthan CET 2026 follows the state reservation norms. Horizontal (cross-category) reservation of 30% applies for women across all categories. Within the women's quota, 8% is reserved for widows and 2% for divorced women (marriage-separated women). If eligible widow candidates are not available, the seats are filled by divorced women of the same category, and vice versa. Reservation for Scheduled Tribes and Scheduled Castes: backlog vacancies are carried forward for up to three subsequent recruitment years if eligible candidates are unavailable in a given year. Outstanding Athletes receive 2% horizontal reservation of total vacancies per notification dated 21.11.2019. Candidates from other states belonging to SC/ST/OBC/MBC/EWS categories are treated as General category for all reservation purposes.

content_sections.heading: Troubleshooting / Common Mistakes
content_sections.text: 1. Submitting the application without completing OTR first. The OTR (One Time Registration) must be completed before the application form can be submitted. Incomplete OTR leads to incomplete or unaccepted applications. Do not confuse the OTR fee payment portal with the application submission page. 2. Using someone else's SSO ID to apply. Applications submitted through another person's SSO ID will be rejected outright. Every applicant must use their own SSO ID and should not share it. 3. Applying under the wrong category. Creamy layer OBC/MBC candidates from Rajasthan and all reserved-category candidates from other states must apply under General category. Applying under an incorrect category cannot be corrected after the editing window closes. 4. Uploading a blurred, filtered, or edited live photo. The live photo must be captured via OTR's live camera - not uploaded from a mobile gallery or edited using any photo editor. Such manipulation leads to automatic disqualification. 5. Darkening more than one option per question or leaving more than 10% of questions without any option darkened. In the OMR-based exam, candidates who leave more than 10% of questions without darkening any of the five circles will be disqualified. If not attempting a question, darken option E . 6. Not keeping a printout of the submitted application. Once Final Submitted, certain fields cannot be changed. Keep the application printout safely for document verification and future reference. 7. Waiting until the last day to apply. Technical delays in OTR payment processing are the applicant's own responsibility. Pay OTR and submit the application well before 23 July 2026.

content_sections.heading: Rajasthan CET 2026 Senior Secondary Level Recruitment 2026 - Important Links

faq.question: Q1. Has RSSB officially released the Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary Level Recruitment Notification 2026?
faq.answer: Yes. RSSB has officially released the Rajasthan CET (Senior Secondary Level) 2026 notification under Advertisement No. 08/2026. The notification is available at rssb.rajasthan.gov.in.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply online for Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary Level 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 23 July 2026 at 11:59 PM (23:59 hrs). No offline applications or late submissions will be accepted after this deadline.

faq.question: Q3. What is the educational qualification required for Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary Level 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must have passed or be appearing in their Senior Secondary (Class 12th) examination or its equivalent. The specific qualifications for individual posts will be mentioned in the department-wise main recruitment notification.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must be at least 18 years and not more than 40 years as on 1 January 2027. Age relaxation is available — 5 years for General women, 5 years for SC/ST/OBC/MBC/EWS men (Rajasthan domicile), and 10 years for SC/ST/OBC/MBC/EWS women (Rajasthan domicile).

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for Rajasthan CET 2026?
faq.answer: The OTR (One Time Registration) fee is ₹600 for General/Creamy Layer OBC-MBC candidates and ₹400 for Non-Creamy Layer OBC-MBC, EWS, SC/ST (Rajasthan domicile), and all Divyang candidates. Candidates who already paid OTR previously do not need to pay again.

faq.question: Q6. When is the Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary Level 2026 exam?
faq.answer: The exam is scheduled from 23 October 2026 to 25 October 2026. It will be conducted in offline OMR-based mode. The Board reserves the right to change the exam dates.

faq.question: Q7. What posts are covered under Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary Level 2026?
faq.answer: The CET covers 14 services including Vanpal (Forest Guard), Constable, Hostel Superintendent, Jamadar Grade-II, Clerk Grade-II, and Junior Assistant across various Rajasthan government departments.

faq.question: Q8. Is the Rajasthan CET 2026 score valid for multiple posts?
faq.answer: Yes. The CET score is valid for three years from the result declaration date. Candidates can use the score to apply for any included post's departmental recruitment exam within this validity period, retaining the best score.

faq.question: Q9. What is the minimum qualifying score in Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must score a minimum of 40% to qualify. SC/ST candidates are eligible for a 5 percentage point relaxation, requiring a minimum of 35%.

faq.question: Q10. Where can I find the official notification and apply for Rajasthan CET Senior Secondary 2026?
faq.answer: The official notification is available at http://rssb.rajasthan.gov.in. Applications must be submitted through the SSO Portal at http://sso.rajasthan.gov.in via the Recruitment Portal.
